programme.title: Inside Out London
programme.desc: Guy Lynn goes undercover to expose the illegal trade in fake taxi licences, while a double-decker bus embarks on the last leg of its journey around the world(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tensions rise between Tiffany and Dotty following the latter's meddling, while Keegan goes looking for work - but when his prospective employer winds him up, the troubled teen makes the mistake of lashing out. Ben urges Mel to stop hassling Sharon, although he is intrigued when she mentions a Mitchell family secret, and Lola is stunned to learn what Jay is planning for Terry's funeral. Tina gets a shock when she spots the woman Shirley punched at the school gates and Habiba throws herself into spying on Ian for Iqra(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Highlights of the fifth annual event in which the team heads out across the UK to meet inspirational youngsters who have been helped by the charity. Matt Baker takes on a challenge laid down by 14-year-old Ethan, who was born with cerebral palsy and is attempting to walk twice as far as he has ever walked in his life(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Daniel realising that Cassie is not really Lexie, the truth about who the housemates are come to light, with Cassie being drugged and held hostage by the friends. However she exploits their weaknesses, insecurities and rivalries in attempt to get them to turn against one another. Meanwhile, Rob has managed to track down Shane, but just as all seems lost he reaches a turning point. Something deep inside him knits together back, and as his mind clears he realises that the cases were never connected(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock make a plan to take a bus journey but not before Jock, posing as Fake Billy, swipes some alcopops from an off-licence and is unsuccessfully pursued by a security guard. Local bully Billy Murphy stumbles upon this chase, and follows his impersonator onto a bus which he proceeds to accidentally commandeer. Pursued by police, the wild ride ironically brings Conor, Jock and the girls closer together - along with the other innocent passengers. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actress and comedian Jo Brand hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, as stand-up Zoe Lyons and newspaper columnist Fintan O'Toole join regular captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: She's delighted family audiences as Mary Poppins and The Sound of Music's Maria, yet retained the power to shock by appearing in the likes of 10 and A Touch of Adultery. Now singer and actress Julie Andrews is taking stock of her life in Home Work: A Memoir of My Hollywood Years, a follow-up to her critically acclaimed memoir, Home. She appears on Graham's sofa to discuss her life and times. Also appearing are Hollywood stars Reese Witherspoon and Jennifer Aniston, both starring in comedy drama series The Morning Show, and Ian McKellen, who is playing a conman in drama thriller The Good Liar(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate a scam that saw its unwitting victim conned out of his life savings and dragged into a money-laundering ring(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell check out a flat in Southfields, London, a maisonette in Wolverhampton and a three-floor terrace house in Ashton-under-Lyme(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A motorist with a genuine claim is left out of pocket when forensic engineers discover that he has used a hammer to grossly exaggerate the damage to his vehicle(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A 12-year-old is left in fear of her life when masked burglars try to break into her house, and a thief does a victory dance after committing a crime. Dominic Littlewood presents(n)

programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw presents from the Westpoint Arena in Exeter as experts Richard Madley and Charlie Ross help two teams to make the most profit at auction(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tina urges Linda to confront Shelley, the woman outside school, to ask her what happened with Ollie. But Linda seems more interested in keeping her onside. Mel continues to provoke Keanu about his secret and surprises everyone with an impressive gift for Louise's birthday. Lola desperately tries to make Jay see sense as the day of Terry's funeral arrives. Tiffany comes up with an idea how she and Keegan can marry quickly(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The 1000th episode of the medical drama sees Jac performing a milestone heart-transplant procedure. However, her personal demons threaten to cloud her judgement, while an old friend returns and is shocked by how much she has changed. The hospital celebrates its 150th birthday with a bonfire party, and Fletch arranges a show-stopping event with some very special guests. Joy is hard to find in the post-op ward, where Ric is still recovering from his brain operation and fears he will never be the same man again(n)

programme.desc: Rob and Cassie are reunited as both their cases draw to a close and arrests are finally made, leaving Rob forced to come to terms with the fact that he is in love with her, but that his actions have destroyed their relationship. As he prepares to leave Dublin for the last time, he heads for the woods at Knocknaree, and watches them being cut down. He encounters Frank who has made a discovery that will shape his future(n)

programme.title: Love and Hate Crime
programme.desc: The case of a young American accused of killing a Hispanic teenager in Florida, a crime that occurred close to Donald Trump's exclusive Mar-a-Lago estate on West Palm Beach. The defendant's lawyer is confident that a local jury will return a not guilty verdict - until an incredible secret is revealed that changes everything(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space tell stories of life on our planet from a brand new perspective, to reveal new discoveries and explore Earth's most remote habitats. In the first episode, satellites follow an elephant family which is struggling through a drought, reveal previously unknown penguin colonies, and discover mysterious ice rings that could put seal pups in danger(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Shocking spelling is the undoing of a chancer attempting to cash in on their travel insurance policy for medical treatment that never took place(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A family suffer a horrific ordeal when masked thieves burst into their home. Plus, a bungling bike thief looking to steal a new set of wheels ends up getting a ride in a police car(n)

programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and David Harper help the red and blue teams find bargains in the antique shops of Dorking in Surrey, while host Anita Manning visits a Roman villa in West Sussex(n)

programme.desc: Jimmi struggles with the aftermath of his mugging. Meanwhile, Al is offered a rare comic from two suspicious window cleaners(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants Michael, Laura and Dubem must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple with a £400,000 budget who want to move to rural Shropshire to improve their young daughter's quality of life and reconnect with family roots. While in the county, Alistair becomes an apprentice under the watchful eye of a master craftsman who handmakes horse harnesses and collars, and then puts them to the test on a horse, ploughing a field the Victorian way(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: A broken coffee table requires expertise to redeem it, bag-maker Neil Wragg uses all of his creativity to rethink an old bicycle and Sarah Moore renovates an old dressing table(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Margie Cooper head to East Sussex for their next auction, and once again Raj gambles a three-figure sum on an item while Margie tries to be clever with her purchases(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: This time, Nick Knowles and the team transforms a home in Bromsgrove. Karis, a 28-year-old woman who has cerebral palsy, currently lives with her mother, Karen, but now her own health is deteriorating and it is making caring for Karis almost impossible. The family have decided that Karis should move in with her older sister Jo, but her entire home is inaccessible for Karis, so the team decides to build a large two-storey extension to make the house more suitable(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For this week's task, the candidates head to a top theme park where they are challenged to create and market their own roller coaster. They will need to design and brand their new attraction for the theme park which will be turned into a 4-D ride that they then pitch to the park's top talent. During the design process, miscommunication costs one team dearly, and later, both teams struggle as they pitch their ideas to the panel. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates from the losing team leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: CEO Adam Frisby signs up social media influencer and body positivity campaigner Chessie King to work on a new collection for In The Style. Adam wants the new collection to be released before the summer season ends, which means the team must work quickly to get their ideas together, design and make the range(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fourth episode, Hannah and Geoff, a couple from London, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Angela Rippon investigates new methods scientists are searching for to fight bacterial infection, revealing how a growing number of bacterial diseases are becoming resistant to the antibiotics currently in use. She meets a GP on the frontline in the fight against infection, and finds out how people can take part in the effort to keep the 'miracle cure' effective(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey for unloved items she can transform. She then turns to upholsterer extraordinaire Sharon O'Connor to make-over a tired armchair, while clockmaker Paul Parry gets creative with a collection of circuit boards. For her own project, Sarah attempts to transform a rusty metal table frame. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Autumn Diaries
programme.desc: John Craven explores the stunning countryside around the historic city of Oxford. Paul Martin learns how Queen guitarist Brian May is playing his part in restoring ancient woodland in Dorset, while Margherita Taylor discovers how one of the UK's most scenic autumnal landscapes could be at risk(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Weasels are often portrayed as villains and associated with bad behaviour. In an attempt to determine whether this assessment is correct, this documentary employs the use of over 50 hidden cameras to follow the adventures of first-time stoat mum Bandita as she raises her kits in a unique weasel wonderland. Cameras capture the creatures interacting, revealing the true nature of the weasel family - feisty and fearless. Narrated by Julie Walters(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Inspector George Gently and The Professionals star Martin Shaw is the latest celebrity to go in search of his roots, and he's particularly interested in finding out more about his paternal grandfather Edwin, who was rarely spoken about by his family. The actor has only one photo of his ancestor, which shows him aged roughly 17 and wearing a military uniform. By zooming in on the picture, Martin's brother was able to identify their grandfather's badge, which suggests he was in the Royal Marines. Now, by meeting with a military historian, Martin tries to fill in the rest of the story, and in the process learns more about Edwin's role in defending Birmingham against German air assaults during the Second World War(n)

programme.desc: Al Murray and Paul Chowdhry join experts James Braxton and Natasha Raskin Sharp on the hunt for bargains to sell at auction. A classic Rolls Royce and an Aston Martin take the two comedians west of London and while Al picks up some antiques with a distinct military flavour, Paul discovers he's a 'hustler' when haggling for collectables and curios. Eventually the pair head to auction in Buckinghamshire(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts as first-round losers the Gladiators and the Darksiders return for one last chance to stay in the competition and reach the second stage. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions in the first of the highest-scoring loser matches, as two teams of students get a second opportunity to earn a place in the next stage of the competition(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Hugh Bonneville narrates a documentary looking at the habits of the family of animals and the skills they have developed to survive a range of environments around the world. The film explores how bears across the world have overcome the challenges of life - from finding food and raising the cubs to confronting rivals and habitat loss - all thanks to brains, brawn and a remarkable ability to adapt. Part of the Natural World strand(n)

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Everyone is delighted when Kevin books a cottage for them to spend half term in the countryside with all their children. But the house is not as nice as the pictures online suggested, and it is not long before tempers begin to fray. Anna Maxwell Martin, Diane Morgan and Paul Ready star(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels from Texas to southern Mexico, meeting a woman who risks her life to take supplies to migrants stranded on the Mexican side of the border in Reynosa, one of the country's most violent cities, which is dominated by a powerful criminal organisation. He also ventures into the rainforest to explore the Maya city of Yaxchilan, and meets the modern-day descendants of this civilisation, who are increasingly marginalised and treated as second-class citizens(n)

programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The second of the two-part documentary begins in 2005 at a moment of crisis for the Conservative Party, following three successive general election defeats. David Cameron, a relatively new MP, and his small group of supporters sought to radically shift the party's direction, taking on veteran opponent David Davis in a leadership election. Here, Cameron re-examines his desire to reshape the party - and how he risked alienating the powerful old guard as he set out to prioritise issues such as environmentalism, social justice and international aid(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the fifth annual event, the team heads out across the UK to meet inspirational youngsters who have been helped by the charity. John Craven leads a walk through the grounds of Perthshire's Scone Palace, Anita Rani is on Cornwall's north coast, Adam Henson is in Glenariff Forest Park in Co Antrim, and Tom Heap is in south Wales(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave and Si cross the state of Arizona in search of the Wild West and make a small detour from Route 66, starting in Monument Valley, to learn more about this striking place. They're invited by a local Navajo police officer to eat with his family at a traditional Navajo barbecue, where a sheep is butchered, grilled and eaten alongside some traditional fry bread. Still riding in the Navajo Nation, the bikers stop at Tsaile College to learn more about rodeo and cook a lamb stew with corn dumplings in homage of the Navajo people they met(n)

programme.desc: DJ, actor and presenter Reggie Yates sets out to trace his ancestry. His mother and father were both born in Ghana, but he never knew his grandparents, and he is keen to know where his surname comes from, believing it might be Dutch. However, as he looks further back, his family tree springs some unexpected surprises(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Johnny Ball, Suzi Ruffell, Nina Wadia and Danny Wallace go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

Half of the competitors are challenged by Marcus Wareing to prepare poached guinea fowl breast with a Sauce Robert, while Monica Galetti sets the rest the challenge of making hand-cut tagliatelle with a lamb ragu. Finally, they must prepare a signature dish to decide who will stay on and who will be eliminated(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef takes a culinary tour of France to discover what Britain's nearest neighbours have to teach us. He begins his trip in Dieppe, where he prepares freshly caught turbot, forages for sea herbs along the Bay of the Somme, and cooks a seafood gratin. He then heads for southern Champagne, a region that was a favourite of Renoir(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Tom Allen is the host for the first in a new run of stand-up showcases, performed in front of a live audience at London's world-famous Hammersmith Apollo. Each show features sets from two of the country's most acclaimed up-and-coming comedians, and this week it is Rosie Allen and Kae Kurd taking to the stage. Performers set to appear in this series include Sindhu Vee, Alex Edelman, Lou Sanders, Darren Harriott, Jessica Fostekew, Stephen Bailey, Desiree Burch, Paul McCaffrey, Jonny Pelham, Ardal O'Hanlon, Sara Barron, Mawaan Rizwan, Guz Khan, Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Rhys James, Sarah Pascoe, Chris McCausland and Flo & Joan(n)

Dr Emily Jones visits 18-month-old Amaya and attaches sensors to her to monitor the physiological changes in her body during a tantrum, while Professor Usha Goswami attaches young babies to highly sensitive brain scanners to see how the infants respond to different types of speech(n)

programme.desc: In a radical social experiment based on a scheme in Germany, 14 young volunteers between the ages of 18 and 24 spend four weeks delivering patient care on the NHS frontline at Royal Derby Hospital, aiming to find out whether volunteers could help relieve the pressures of staff on the wards and make a positive difference to patient care in the NHS. As the volunteers embark on their final week of the scheme, The Royal Derby Hospital senior staff assess the success of the experiment. Meanwhile, Michael struggles to perform personal care for patients in the elderly care ward, and the volunteers' personal prejudices are challenged when a homeless patient who is suffering from alcoholism is brought into the hospital(n)

programme.desc: New findings by the Deafness Cognition and Language project that show people born deaf can use their auditory cortex for working memory. With voiceover(n)

programme.desc: Comedian and actor Billy Connolly sets out to fill in the gaps in his own family history. Starting in Glasgow, the city of Billy's birth, he hopes to find out more about his mother's relatives. He believes they hailed from Ireland, but records concerning his great-great-grandmother only deepen the mystery. Billy also heads to India, where he unearths details of the parts two of his ancestors played in historical events(n)

programme.desc: Johnny Ball, Suzi Ruffell, Nina Wadia and Danny Wallace test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball presents more backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing. Plus, Ian Waite casts his eye over the couples' latest training footage(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests with interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest news on Brexit(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton explores lochs close to the densely populated Central Belt, beginning on the banks of the Lake of Menteith and visiting Loch Ard, Loch Arklet, Loch Katrine and Loch Achray, before ending by climbing Ben Venue in the Trossachs(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first three chefs are challenged to recreate Marcus' crab omelette Arnold Bennett, before the second trio take on Monica's puff pastry and vanilla mousseline layered dessert. Moving into the second round, the six chefs are then tasked to impress the judges with a signature dish of their own design(n)

programme.title: Guilt
programme.desc: Max tries to derail private detective Kenny, a drunken no-hoper who used to work for him, but the case has given the investigator the purpose he needs to get back on track. To make matters worse, Max is also dealing with threats from Walter's neighbour Sheila, who reveals she witnessed something on the night in question. Later, Max's wife Claire's grows suspicious of his actions and cracks begin to show in their marriage. Thriller, starring Mark Bonnar, Jamie Sives, Emun Elliott, Ellie Haddington, Ruth Bradley and Sian Brooke(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: The companion programme to The Apprentice, with Tom Allen talking to the sixth candidate to be ejected from the competition. The host is also joined by a panel of experts and fans, who offer an entertaining analysis of this week's task(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine investigate a rural case of murder and moonlighting in Ireland in 1894 resulting in the death of James Donovan. With little evidence, the police rounded up several known criminals from the surrounding area, and John Twiss from Co Kerry, over 16 miles from the murder scene, was tried and convicted. He was hanged in February 1895, protesting his innocence(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: A Stitch in Time
programme.desc: Amber Butchart explores the life of Dido Belle, the 18th-century daughter of an enslaved African woman brought up at Kenwood House in London(n)

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Monty Halls explores the extraordinary ecosystem that covers an area larger than Great Britain, revealing that only seven per cent of it is coral reef. The rest is a variety of interconnected habitats, including hundreds of islands, mangrove swamps, deep-water gardens, vast sand flats, meadows of sea grass and what is said to be the world's oldest jungle. All are interconnected by a giant deep-water lagoon - and all are full of amazing wildlife(n)

programme.title: A British Guide to the End of the World
programme.desc: Documentary charting the UK's efforts to prepare for the nuclear age, from the first bomb tests to the potentially futile preparations for attack during the Cold War. Featuring archive footage and testimonies from people directly involved in the nation's nuclear story - from conscripted soldiers attending the early nuclear tests in the South Pacific to servicemen, volunteers and civil servants involved in the planning of how Britain might manage in the event of a nuclear catastrophe(n)

programme.title: The Story of China
programme.desc: Michael Wood's exploration of the history of China concludes with the impact of its revolutions, of which there were three between 1850 and 1950. Michael heads into the wild mountain villages in the south of the country where the Taiping Rebellion began and lasted from 1850 to 1864, fought between the established Manchu-led Qing dynasty and the Heavenly Kingdom of Peace, led by Hong Xiuquan. The Boxer Rebellion at the turn of the 20th century developed against the disruption caused by the growth of foreign spheres of influence in China, while Michael talks to a survivor of the Nanjing Massacre, when Japanese troops took over the city in 1937(n)

programme.title: A Call from the Hostage Takers
programme.desc: Documentary about a little-known story from the Iranian hostage crisis in 1979, when the captors issued an invitation to another 50 Americans to visit to hear their reasons(n)

programme.title: Genius of the Modern World
programme.desc: Historian Bettany Hughes travels across Europe in the footsteps of the German philosopher Friedrich Nietzsche. His uncompromising and often brutal ideas smashed the comfortable assumptions of religion, morality and science, yet appropriated by the Nazis, his work is among the most devastatingly manipulated and misinterpreted in history. His philosophical quest led him to isolation and ultimately madness, but his ideas helped shape the intellectual landscape of the modern world(n)

programme.title: Apples, Pears and Paint: How to Make a Still Life Painting
programme.desc: Examining the history of the art form that depicts fruit, flowers and domestic objects, from the Xenia murals at Pompeii through to the cubist works of Picasso. The film also takes a look at the work of some of the artists synonymous with the genre, including Chardin and Cezanne, and features contributions from Bettany Hughes, Janina Ramirez and Andrew Graham-Dixon(n)

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Monty Halls explores the extraordinary ecosystem that covers an area larger than Great Britain, revealing that only seven per cent of it is coral reef. The rest is a variety of interconnected habitats, including hundreds of islands, mangrove swamps, deep-water gardens, vast sand flats, meadows of sea grass and what is said to be the world's oldest jungle. All are interconnected by a giant deep-water lagoon - and all are full of amazing wildlife(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: A Stitch in Time
programme.desc: Amber Butchart explores the lives of historical figures through the clothes they wore, focusing on Edward of Woodstock (1330-1376), the so-called Black Prince. She reveals how the effigy of the son of Edward III provides many pointers as to the personality and attitude of this warrior prince both in terms of its heraldic symbolism and its flamboyance(n)

programme.title: Dive, Dive, Dive!
programme.desc: Robert Llewellyn investigates the appeal of films set on board submarines, travelling along the River Medway to find a beached Cold War Russian nuclear vessel, and visiting abandoned Second World War German U-boat pens on the French coast. He recalls the events that inspired movies including The Hunt for Red October, and reveals the role played by Walt Disney in promoting atomic submarines(n)

programme.title: Rich Hall's Red Menace
programme.desc: The comedian takes a look back at the Cold War from perspectives on both sides of the Iron Curtain, from the political machinations to the daily lives of ordinary people. He discovers how paranoia about the enemy seeped into every aspect of society in both the USA and the USSR, from fear of influences in pop culture to sporting drama(n)

programme.title: Dr Strangelove - or How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Bomb (1964)
programme.desc: An insane general brings the world to the brink of disaster by launching a nuclear attack on Russia. As the president strives to avert the crisis and a British officer tries to bring the general to his senses, a crazed Nazi scientist prepares his plans for life after the war. Stanley Kubrick's satirical comedy, starring Peter Sellers in three roles, alongside Sterling Hayden, George C Scott and Slim Pickens(n)

programme.title: The Silent War
programme.desc: Part one of two. British, American and Soviet submarine crews recall their experiences of the Cold War, when their vessels engaged in perilous games of cat-and-mouse as each side battled to gain the tactical and technological advantage. The programme explores close encounters between the opposing navies that put lives at risk and servicemen talk about the pressures of lengthy underwater patrols that drove them to the edge of their physical and mental limits. Haydn Gwynne narrates(n)

programme.title: The Silent War
programme.desc: Part two of two. During the Cold War, there was a constant struggle to gain technological advantages over the enemy, and the Soviets developed submarines that were ever more sophisticated - bigger, faster and more luxurious than any developed by the West. British, American and Soviet submariners reveal how the underwater arms race took ballistic missiles beneath the Arctic ice and recall how it nearly ended in nuclear disaster at sea(n)

programme.title: A Stitch in Time
programme.desc: Amber Butchart explores the lives of historical figures through the clothes they wore, focusing on Edward of Woodstock (1330-1376), the so-called Black Prince. She reveals how the effigy of the son of Edward III provides many pointers as to the personality and attitude of this warrior prince both in terms of its heraldic symbolism and its flamboyance(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days

programme.title: A Stitch in Time
programme.desc: Amber Butchart explores the lives of historical figures through the clothes they wore. Here she focuses on the ill-fated French queen, Marie Antoinette, and learns more about her lack of political judgment and her desire to be released from the formality of court life(n)

programme.title: Six Wives with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy witnesses the impact of Anne Boleyn on Henry VIII's affections, uncovering private moments that were reported by eyewitnesses or recorded in historical documents. The key moments during their marriage are recreated, as Anne falls from favour in court and is eventually executed, despite protestations of innocence regarding the rumours of her infidelity. As soon as Anne is dead, Henry marries Jane Seymour, who fulfils her most important role as Queen - giving birth to the healthy son that Henry has craved for so long(n)

programme.desc: Dr Bendor Grosvenor finds two anonymous landscape paintings at Birmingham Art Gallery, which he believes may be by the Flemish painter Brueghel(n)

programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke discover the art and architecture of Barcelona, seeking out the Romanesque frescoes that inspired a young Miró and the designs of an engineer who arguably put more of a stamp on the city than its star architect, Antoni Gaudí(n)

programme.desc: In the conclusion of the two-part programme, biographer AN Wilson continues to explore the personal life of Queen Victoria through her journals and letters, examining the monarch's closest relationships in the four decades following Prince Albert's death. programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: My Dog Ears follows the journey of 12-year-old Joe who is hoping to receive a very precious gift - a dog that will become his ears. Joe has been profoundly deaf since he was a baby and a specially trained hearing dog could bring him the independence and confidence he so craves. Only 30 children in the UK have one of these dogs and Joe is next on the waiting list, but his journey isn't straightforward. Joe also has a Titanium leg and needs an operation to help him walk before he can receive his dog. programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tensions rise between Tiffany and Dotty following the latter's meddling, while Keegan goes looking for work - but when his prospective employer winds him up, the troubled teen makes the mistake of lashing out. Ben urges Mel to stop hassling Sharon, although he is intrigued when she mentions a Mitchell family secret, and Lola is stunned to learn what Jay is planning for Terry's funeral. Tina gets a shock when she spots the woman Shirley punched at the school gates and Habiba throws herself into spying on Ian for Iqra(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actors and comedians Liz Carr and Miles Jupp, actor and writer Samson Kayo and broadcaster Anneka Rice.join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Daniel realising that Cassie is not really Lexie, the truth about who the housemates are come to light, with Cassie being drugged and held hostage by the friends. However she exploits their weaknesses, insecurities and rivalries in attempt to get them to turn against one another. Meanwhile, Rob has managed to track down Shane, but just as all seems lost he reaches a turning point. Something deep inside him knits together back, and as his mind clears he realises that the cases were never connected(n)

programme.title: Tourist Trap
programme.desc: Wow! Wales promotes genealogy tourism and all of the staff do DNA tests to find out how Welsh they are, while the 'King of Wales' pays a visit from America. Comedy, starring Sally Phillips, with guest appearances by Scott Quinnell and Stuart Milligan(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock make a plan to take a bus journey but not before Jock, posing as Fake Billy, swipes some alcopops from an off-licence and is unsuccessfully pursued by a security guard. Local bully Billy Murphy stumbles upon this chase, and follows his impersonator onto a bus which he proceeds to accidentally commandeer. Pursued by police, the wild ride ironically brings Conor, Jock and the girls closer together - along with the other innocent passengers. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actress and comedian Jo Brand hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, as stand-up Zoe Lyons and newspaper columnist Fintan O'Toole join regular captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: She's delighted family audiences as Mary Poppins and The Sound of Music's Maria, yet retained the power to shock by appearing in the likes of 10 and A Touch of Adultery. Now singer and actress Julie Andrews is taking stock of her life in Home Work: A Memoir of My Hollywood Years, a follow-up to her critically acclaimed memoir, Home. She appears on Graham's sofa to discuss her life and times. Also appearing are Hollywood stars Reese Witherspoon and Jennifer Aniston, both starring in comedy drama series The Morning Show, and Ian McKellen, who is playing a conman in drama thriller The Good Liar(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tina urges Linda to confront Shelley, the woman outside school, to ask her what happened with Ollie. But Linda seems more interested in keeping her onside. Mel continues to provoke Keanu about his secret and surprises everyone with an impressive gift for Louise's birthday. Lola desperately tries to make Jay see sense as the day of Terry's funeral arrives. Tiffany comes up with an idea how she and Keegan can marry quickly(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The 1000th episode of the medical drama sees Jac performing a milestone heart-transplant procedure. However, her personal demons threaten to cloud her judgement, while an old friend returns and is shocked by how much she has changed. The hospital celebrates its 150th birthday with a bonfire party, and Fletch arranges a show-stopping event with some very special guests. Joy is hard to find in the post-op ward, where Ric is still recovering from his brain operation and fears he will never be the same man again(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: Rob and Cassie are reunited as both their cases draw to a close and arrests are finally made, leaving Rob forced to come to terms with the fact that he is in love with her, but that his actions have destroyed their relationship. As he prepares to leave Dublin for the last time, he heads for the woods at Knocknaree, and watches them being cut down. He encounters Frank who has made a discovery that will shape his future(n)

programme.title: Beauty Queen and Single
programme.desc: Shelley, a businesswoman who was a finalist in 2010's Miss Wales, has not gone out in public with no make-up for years - but now faces the challenge of five dates without it. As always, stylist Nathan is on hand to help her build up her confidence and offers her his own personal twist on the classic little black dress(n)

programme.title: Love and Hate Crime
programme.desc: The case of a young American accused of killing a Hispanic teenager in Florida, a crime that occurred close to Donald Trump's exclusive Mar-a-Lago estate on West Palm Beach. The defendant's lawyer is confident that a local jury will return a not guilty verdict - until an incredible secret is revealed that changes everything(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space tell stories of life on our planet from a brand new perspective, to reveal new discoveries and explore Earth's most remote habitats. In the first episode, satellites follow an elephant family which is struggling through a drought, reveal previously unknown penguin colonies, and discover mysterious ice rings that could put seal pups in danger(n)

programme.desc: A broken coffee table requires expertise to redeem it, bag-maker Neil Wragg uses all of his creativity to rethink an old bicycle and Sarah Moore renovates an old dressing table(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: This time, Nick Knowles and the team transforms a home in Bromsgrove. Karis, a 28-year-old woman who has cerebral palsy, currently lives with her mother, Karen, but now her own health is deteriorating and it is making caring for Karis almost impossible. The family have decided that Karis should move in with her older sister Jo, but her entire home is inaccessible for Karis, so the team decides to build a large two-storey extension to make the house more suitable(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For this week's task, the candidates head to a top theme park where they are challenged to create and market their own roller coaster. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates from the losing team leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. programme.title: EastEnders

Mel continues to provoke Keanu about his secret and surprises everyone with an impressive gift for Louise's birthday. Lola desperately tries to make Jay see sense as the day of Terry's funeral arrives. Tiffany comes up with an idea how she and Keegan can marry quickly(n)

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Lenny is stunned to be suddenly released from prison, but discovers his future lies in the hands of a surprising person from his past. Lou discovers the police are looking for a surveillance camera at Amber Cabs and knows she needs to get hold of it before her colleagues do, while Kelly-Marie competes with Scarlett for Callum's affections(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: Rob and Cassie are reunited as both their cases draw to a close and arrests are finally made, leaving Rob forced to come to terms with the fact that he is in love with her, but that his actions have destroyed their relationship. As he prepares to leave Dublin for the last time, he heads for the woods at Knocknaree, and watches them being cut down. He encounters Frank who has made a discovery that will shape his future(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The 1000th episode of the medical drama sees Jac performing a milestone heart-transplant procedure. However, her personal demons threaten to cloud her judgement, while an old friend returns and is shocked by how much she has changed. The hospital celebrates its 150th birthday with a bonfire party, and Fletch arranges a show-stopping event with some very special guests. Joy is hard to find in the post-op ward, where Ric is still recovering from his brain operation and fears he will never be the same man again(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: A broken coffee table requires expertise to redeem it, bag-maker Neil Wragg uses all of his creativity to rethink an old bicycle and Sarah Moore renovates an old dressing table(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: This time, Nick Knowles and the team transforms a home in Bromsgrove. Karis, a 28-year-old woman who has cerebral palsy, currently lives with her mother, Karen, but now her own health is deteriorating and it is making caring for Karis almost impossible. The family have decided that Karis should move in with her older sister Jo, but her entire home is inaccessible for Karis, so the team decides to build a large two-storey extension to make the house more suitable(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For this week's task, the candidates head to a top theme park where they are challenged to create and market their own roller coaster. They will need to design and brand their new attraction for the theme park which will be turned into a 4-D ride that they then pitch to the park's top talent. During the design process, miscommunication costs one team dearly, and later, both teams struggle as they pitch their ideas to the panel. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates from the losing team leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: CEO Adam Frisby signs up social media influencer and body positivity campaigner Chessie King to work on a new collection for In The Style. Adam wants the new collection to be released before the summer season ends, which means the team must work quickly to get their ideas together, design and make the range(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fourth episode, Hannah and Geoff, a couple from London, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Angela Rippon investigates new methods scientists are searching for to fight bacterial infection, revealing how a growing number of bacterial diseases are becoming resistant to the antibiotics currently in use. She meets a GP on the frontline in the fight against infection, and finds out how people can take part in the effort to keep the 'miracle cure' effective(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste are on the trail of criminals extorting money from unsuspecting victims from online dating scams, beginning with a man who trick a businesswoman out of a total of almost £60,000 over the course of a few months(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts looks round a one-bedroom flat in Tunbridge Wells and Martel Maxwell examines the state of a former supermarket building in Derbyshire and a mid-terrace in Liverpool(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty presents the programme exposing cases of insurance fraud. CCTV cameras are the undoing of a dangerous driver as the true nature of his run-in with a bus is caught on camera, while a woman's hopes of a pay-out after a work-related back injury are set back when footage emerges of her bungee jumping and jet skiing(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Local people join forces with the police to help catch a thief when a pub landlady has money stolen that was meant for her seriously ill daughter. Dominic Littlewood presents(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Roo Irvine help two teams look for items in an antiques centre in the Lancashire town of Eccleston, with Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi is attacked, while Daniel has a huge revelation and Rob encounters a familiar face, but is worried that the person may have gone off the rails since they last met(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz, with Dubem, Sarah, and Melissa choosing between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton looks for property on Anglesey for a couple who have a £475,000 budget, and also learns about the importance of preserving the island's ancient woodland(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms items reclaimed from a skip in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, including a dressing table and mirrors and an old rusty water tank(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Margie Cooper set off on a search around the Home Counties, buying items along the way to sell at an auction in Bourne End, Buckinghamshire(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Family Footsteps
programme.desc: Gavin Andrews takes the Smyths back to 1711 to reveal family ties to a woman who was accused and convicted in a notorious trial, and finds a fascinating link to one of the most dramatic early chapters in the story of the Ulster-Scots people(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tensions rise between Tiffany and Dotty following the latter's meddling, while Keegan goes looking for work - but when his prospective employer winds him up, the troubled teen makes the mistake of lashing out. Ben urges Mel to stop hassling Sharon, although he is intrigued when she mentions a Mitchell family secret, and Lola is stunned to learn what Jay is planning for Terry's funeral. Tina gets a shock when she spots the woman Shirley punched at the school gates and Habiba throws herself into spying on Ian for Iqra(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Highlights of the fifth annual event in which the team heads out across the UK to meet inspirational youngsters who have been helped by the charity. Matt Baker takes on a challenge laid down by 14-year-old Ethan, who was born with cerebral palsy and is attempting to walk twice as far as he has ever walked in his life(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Daniel realising that Cassie is not really Lexie, the truth about who the housemates are come to light, with Cassie being drugged and held hostage by the friends. However she exploits their weaknesses, insecurities and rivalries in attempt to get them to turn against one another. Meanwhile, Rob has managed to track down Shane, but just as all seems lost he reaches a turning point. Something deep inside him knits together back, and as his mind clears he realises that the cases were never connected(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary about a Belfast drag performer diagnosed with HIV, following Matthew Cavan as he demonstrates that people with the virus can lead normal, healthy, happy lives. In the course of the programme, he discusses a change in his medication with a consultant and meets John O'Doherty, director of The Rainbow Centre in Belfast, to find out why the stigma around HIV is so damaging(n)

programme.title: Pop Goes Northern Ireland
programme.desc: The music and news stories of 1989, the year of the Deal bombing, which killed 11 members of the Royal Marine band at their barracks. A series of deaths led to accusations of both IRA operatives in the Irish police force and collusion between security forces and Loyalist paramilitaries. The year also saw the release of the Guildford Four, after their convictions were declared a miscarriage of justice at the appeal court(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock make a plan to take a bus journey but not before Jock, posing as Fake Billy, swipes some alcopops from an off-licence and is unsuccessfully pursued by a security guard. Local bully Billy Murphy stumbles upon this chase, and follows his impersonator onto a bus which he proceeds to accidentally commandeer. Pursued by police, the wild ride ironically brings Conor, Jock and the girls closer together - along with the other innocent passengers. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actress and comedian Jo Brand hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, as stand-up Zoe Lyons and newspaper columnist Fintan O'Toole join regular captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: She's delighted family audiences as Mary Poppins and The Sound of Music's Maria, yet retained the power to shock by appearing in the likes of 10 and A Touch of Adultery. Now singer and actress Julie Andrews is taking stock of her life in Home Work: A Memoir of My Hollywood Years, a follow-up to her critically acclaimed memoir, Home. She appears on Graham's sofa to discuss her life and times. Also appearing are Hollywood stars Reese Witherspoon and Jennifer Aniston, both starring in comedy drama series The Morning Show, and Ian McKellen, who is playing a conman in drama thriller The Good Liar(n)

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Over 30 years on, the scale and magnitude of the Piper Alpha disaster continues to shock. Iona Ballantyne's father Bob was a well-known spokesman for survivors and their families, and a delayed response to grief was felt strongly by Iona years after his death. To understand and process her own thoughts and loss she visits other families, who like her, were direct witnesses to the consequences of the disaster and the changes it forced upon them(n)

programme.title: HMS Timbertown
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the experiences of 102 sailors from Lewis who were interned in a PoW camp in the Netherlands for the duration of the First World War. Presenter Angela MacLean retraces the journey the men took to the camp, which became known as HMS Timbertown, and uncovers the realities of their lives there - from the highs of football matches, to the lows of failed escape attempts and execution(n)

programme.title: Fifty Shades of Green with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: To celebrate half a century spent in gardens around the world, the broadcaster visits his favourite horticultural locations around the UK with friends and experts who share his passions. He meets food writer Mary Berry at his favourite vegetable plot, goes inside Prince Charles' garden at Highgrove, takes a tour of Griff Rhys Jones' epic space and and strokes the hallowed turf on Wimbledon's Centre Court. Alan also returns to the allotment where, as a toddler, he was first introduced to gardening(n)

programme.title: Riding a Dream
programme.desc: The remarkable story of 18-year-old British Muslim woman Khadijah Mellah, and her journey to the 2019 Magnolia Cup. From her inner city riding school to one of the most prestigious horse racing events in the world, this film follows her ups and downs as she gets ready for the race of her life(n)

Ten years on, Jeremy hears a report from neighbouring Nepal of a mysterious attack that sounds strangely familiar - a lone fisherman on a mountain river dragged off his feet and into the water by an unseen creature. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Gina Ward makes a surprise return to Aidensfield and Bellamy encounters a key informant who might come in useful - but finds himself in trouble when the man disappears before he can offer a tip-off about an imminent armed robbery. Meanwhile, Vernon acquires a bargain miniature railway which he hopes will impress the new estate manager, and it emerges Dr Liz Merrick may have feelings for Lord Ashfordly's recently appointed land agent. Tricia Penrose, Mark Jordon and Geoffrey Hughes star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective looks forward to a quiet Christmas, but is disturbed by the Foreign Office when an Egyptian prince loses a famous ruby - after making the mistake of lending it to a mysterious woman. Guest starring Stephanie Cole, Nigel Le Vaillant and Tariq Alibai(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the death of a racehorse trainer and suspect an aristocratic family's troubled history holds the key to the mystery - until a second killing makes them think again. Groom Jake Foley seems the obvious suspect, but as events unfold, the detectives' inquiries gradually focus instead on the residents of stately Bantling Hall. Detective drama, starring John Nettles(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel can't get Gill's remarks about her fling with Will out of her head, so ducks out of attending an awards ceremony with him, fearing their colleagues will believe she's sleeping her way to the top. He finally decides that dating her is too much like hard work and so announces they are no longer an item - a move that leaves Rachel gutted and regretting her behaviour. Janet isn't happy either - it's clear her daughter Elise is about to have her heart broken by Eleanor's son Ade. The duo are soon distracted from their woes by a new case involving the murder of a landlord and his wife who were shot at close range in their pub(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet voices her concerns to Dodson about Gill's drinking, hoping it can be dealt with amicably, so she's unnerved when her observations are taken more seriously than she intended. Meanwhile, just as Rachel feels like she's getting to grips with the sergeant's job, she makes a fatal error that puts Janet and Chris in danger. The body of a man who disappeared 15 years previously is found on the moors. Inquiries lead to a nearby farm, where the detectives encounter something much more sinister than they could have anticipated(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour detective Jack Frost investigates a woman's near-fatal shooting. The victim was all set to testify against a notorious drugs baron accused of murdering one of his employees, but the appearance of a contract killer makes her think again - and it appears someone on Jack's team is leaking information. Meanwhile, the station is turned upside down in the hunt for a missing lottery ticket. David Jason, Michael McKell and Bruce Alexander star(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve is confident he will be able to talk Alison round, Ivy is relieved when Don returns, and many of the locals gather at number seven to see in the New Year(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ivy is happy when Don goes to confession, Jim and Liz agree to take things one step at a time, and Jack fears that Vera will upset the Hortons and they will never see Tommy again(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The flames of Frank's suspicion are fanned, while at Emmerdale, tempers flare when Annie makes an announcement(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack makes Robert fear the worst, and Lorraine's news is music to her mother's ears(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

Tricia Penrose, Mark Jordon and Geoffrey Hughes star(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Mum wins a holiday for two in sunny Spain, so Stan, Olive and Arthur compete to do the chores in the hope that she will invite them to accompany her. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Doris Hare(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: With Arthur having been gone for six months, Stan accompanies his sister Olive to the divorce courts. Reg Varney and Anna Karen star(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin borrow Mr Roper's car to drive to Southampton for a football match. However, the vehicle has similar qualities to its unreliable owner and soon breaks down leaving the duo having to summon help from an unexpected quarter. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Merrick faces a testing time when a terrified hospital patient claims her husband is trying to kill her, but Merton seems reluctant to get involved, while Vernon picks up a bargain all-purpose pest-control solution, enlisting David's help in his eradication mission. Gina's cousin Diane arrives to help her and Phil with the wedding preparations - much to the delight of PC Crane, who is instantly smitten. Rural drama, starring Aislin McGuckin, Tricia Penrose and Mark Jordon(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack swallows his pride, while Turner is proud to bring his new friend home. At Home Farm, it is the end of the world for Elizabeth as Frank gives her some devastating news(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Windsors are finding country life a strain, while Julie's anxiety brings back memories for Nick. Annie is on the move, and the Sugdens are reconciled by a special gift(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des offers Jack his help in demanding access to Tommy. Curly fends off rumours of redundancy and is stunned when Reg tells him the rumours are true. Don writes Denise a letter(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Don makes an effort to be nice to Ivy. Curly receives the redundancy list from Head Office, and it includes Maureen's name. Bet is pleased when Charlie asks her out for dinner(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Merrick faces a testing time when a terrified hospital patient claims her husband is trying to kill her, but Merton seems reluctant to get involved, while Vernon picks up a bargain all-purpose pest-control solution, enlisting David's help in his eradication mission. Gina's cousin Diane arrives to help her and Phil with the wedding preparations - much to the delight of PC Crane, who is instantly smitten. Rural drama, starring Aislin McGuckin, Tricia Penrose and Mark Jordon(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Bullard asks Barnaby to investigate the disappearance of a friend's daughter who was a member of the Oblong Foundation, a cult based at Malham Hall. Jones goes under cover as a new recruit to solve the mystery, but he and Barnaby soon start digging into an old case involving the death of a couple in a boat explosion, which could be linked to the missing woman. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with Will Knightley, Kevin Doyle, Holly Aird, Barbara Flynn and John Woodvine(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the victim of a frenzied attack is found in the woods, the team initially wonders whether it is random or related to the location - a shrine to a local girl who recently committed suicide there. However, Banks has a theory of his own. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. When a woman commits suicide, her grieving father relies on violence to procure answers as he investigates his daughter's death. She left a note confessing to Damon Horsley's murder, but Banks is suspicious, and his hunch is confirmed when it appears that the woman's death is not a simple suicide and someone else may have been involved. The pressure is on as the team gathers evidence and tries to solve two murders. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz and Jack Deam(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Julie's past seems to be catching up with her, Jayesh is forced to put recent events behind him, while at Emmerdale, Sarah tells Jack their lives will never be the same again(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic is plotting revenge, Lynn kicks Chris when he is down, and Michael almost bites the hand that feeds him(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim apologises to Liz for putting her in an awkward position. Reg warns Curly he intends to go over his head to secure Maureen's job. Alf is proud to be asked to be the new Mayor(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Audrey wants Alf to agree to be Mayor, the twins are delighted when Liz and Jim tell them they are back together. Kevin demands a rise from Mike(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve is forced to cover for Phil when the father-to-be discovers Gina has gone into labour two months early and rushes to the hospital to help with the nerve-racking delivery. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A depressive gambler is found dead, the apparent victim of suicide. Dr Jane Moore, who once treated the man, is adamant he wouldn't have taken his own life - a theory backed up by the post-mortem. Thrilled to be working with Jane again, Barnaby investigates the case - and it isn't long before another of the doctor's patients is killed. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Isla Blair, Jan Ravens and Daniel Casey(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack is offered the job of bus inspector and Stan is promised extra overtime, for which he will not have to work - as well as a glamorous conductress. Reg Varney and Bob Grant star(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel and Bet do battle at the sales, Vera tries to contact the Hortons in vain, and Ivy reports Don as a missing person to the police(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack is concerned for Vera as she refuses to eat or sleep, Des receives his decree nisi, Don contacts Bet to see how Ivy is, and Vicky wants Steve to go away with her(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank cannot believe what Zoe is telling him, Pollard panics when Elizabeth reveals her plan, and Archie's request gives Rachel the wrong idea(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: At the barbecue, Rachel loses a friend, Vic is fuming when Scott loses his balance, and Chris is lost for words when he is finally cut down to size(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A recently widowed woman arrives in Aidensfield wanting to bury her husband in the family grave, but the deceased's brother refuses to co-operate because of an old feud. programme.desc: Stan and Jack eavesdrop on Blakey's medical - and completely misinterpret its findings. Comedy, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy takes over Robin's poker game when he is rushed into hospital. Unfortunately, she does not fully understand the rules and puts the rent money at risk. Comedy, with Paula Wilcox and Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy(n)

programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Blakey cuts the canteen's opening hours, but when he sacks one of the staff, things really turn nasty. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des offers Jack his help in demanding access to Tommy. Curly fends off rumours of redundancy and is stunned when Reg tells him the rumours are true. Don writes Denise a letter(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Don makes an effort to be nice to Ivy. Curly receives the redundancy list from Head Office, and it includes Maureen's name. Bet is pleased when Charlie asks her out for dinner(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack swallows his pride, while Turner is proud to bring his new friend home. At Home Farm, it is the end of the world for Elizabeth as Frank gives her some devastating news(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Windsors are finding country life a strain, while Julie's anxiety brings back memories for Nick. Annie is on the move, and the Sugdens are reconciled by a special gift(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Merrick faces a testing time when a terrified hospital patient claims her husband is trying to kill her, but Merton seems reluctant to get involved, while Vernon picks up a bargain all-purpose pest-control solution, enlisting David's help in his eradication mission. Gina's cousin Diane arrives to help her and Phil with the wedding preparations - much to the delight of PC Crane, who is instantly smitten. Rural drama, starring Aislin McGuckin, Tricia Penrose and Mark Jordon(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan is having difficulty supporting the whole family on his meagre earnings. Comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Olive and Mum go on a spending spree, leaving Stan to foot the bill. Comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin regrets sampling Mr Roper's home brew when he is breathalysed during a driving lesson with Chrissy. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve is forced to cover for Phil when the father-to-be discovers Gina has gone into labour two months early and rushes to the hospital to help with the nerve-racking delivery. Meanwhile, Merton is excited when it appears a drug dealer could soon be brought to justice. Drama, starring Mark Jordon, Tricia Penrose and Duncan Bell(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Julie's past seems to be catching up with her, Jayesh is forced to put recent events behind him, while at Emmerdale, Sarah tells Jack their lives will never be the same again(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic is plotting revenge, Lynn kicks Chris when he is down, and Michael almost bites the hand that feeds him(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim apologises to Liz for putting her in an awkward position. Reg warns Curly he intends to go over his head to secure Maureen's job. Alf is proud to be asked to be the new Mayor(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Audrey wants Alf to agree to be Mayor, the twins are delighted when Liz and Jim tell them they are back together. Kevin demands a rise from Mike(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.desc: A depressive gambler is found dead, the apparent victim of suicide. Dr Jane Moore, who once treated the man, is adamant he wouldn't have taken his own life - a theory backed up by the post-mortem. Thrilled to be working with Jane again, Barnaby investigates the case - and it isn't long before another of the doctor's patients is killed. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Isla Blair, Jan Ravens and Daniel Casey(n)

programme.desc: Sabrina Grant and Anna Richardson help two families cut their shopping costs, revealing the secrets of the supermarket trade and the truth behind price comparison adverts. programme.title: The British Tribe Next Door
programme.desc: Scarlett's 89-year-old grandma comes to stay, leading to a discussion of how elderly people are treated in Britain that shocks the Himba community. Mark is shocked to discover how much independence and responsibility Himba children have, leading him to question if his own parents are over-protective. A pregnant woman in the village goes into labour, giving the Moffatts a taste of traditional Himba childbirth(n)

programme.title: The End of the ****ing World

programme.title: The End of the ****ing World
programme.desc: Alyssa is forced to spend the night in a remote motel where the staff are not used to receiving guests(n)

programme.title: Shut-Ins: Britain's Fattest People
programme.desc: Documentary following the struggle of three severely morbidly obese people as they attempt to regain control of their lives before it is too late. Claire has been bed-bound for the last six years. Trapped by her 40-stone body, she can only see the world through the window of the room where she spends her life. Claire has two daughters - Tracey and Jasmine - who need to do nearly everything for her. Tracey has two young children and a home of her own, so the burden of care falls mainly on 19-year-old Jasmine. The programme tracks the family's journey as local surgeon Nick Carter battles to get Clare the help she needs(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay continues his travels across America saving failing restaurants from disaster. In the first of two visits to Mill Street Bistro in Norwalk, Ohio, the chef encounters Joe Nagy, who claims he has the best menu from New York to Los Angeles. However, his servers and diners tell a different story, and his resistance to change leads to some explosive confrontations(n)

programme.title: Smuggled
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary exploring the effectiveness of Britain's border security, with people attempting to smuggle themselves into the UK from a variety of locations around continental Europe. From boats to lorries, and ferries to car boots, all of the contributors attempt routes that have been used by those seeking to enter the country clandestinely and by people-traffickers and drug dealers(n)

programme.title: Nachom-ia Kumpasar (2015)
programme.desc: A young musician playing in Bombay's nightclubs in the 1960s returns to his home in Goa in search of a singer for his band. He soon finds the perfect candidate, and falls madly in love with her as he sets out to make her a star. Romantic musical, starring Vijay Maury and Palomi Ghosh. In Konkani(n)

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: A look at the roles of Boris Johnson's special adviser Dominic Cummings and Jeremy Corbyn's communications and strategy director Seamus Milne, with both seen as disruptors who appear to sit outside the mainstream of their parties. Dispatches investigates concerns about the power they wield, looking at ways in which they have been instrumental in the attempts to deliver Brexit and how these unelected officials are looking to shape Britain's future?(n)
