programme.desc: US Navy lieutenant Paul Booth is shot dead while sitting on a park bench. It turns out that the last people he called were his boss, his wife and Penelope Langston - a rebellious academic who also happens to be McGee's grandmother. She says that the victim was an old student of hers who kept in touch, but later reveals she was recruited to work on a special project during the Vietnam War by the head of a top-secret think tank Booth was a member of. Guest starring Lily Tomlin, with Mark Harmon and Sean Murray(n)

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice advises a basement flat owner who is unable to find a buyer for her home in Stoke Newington, north London. Sweeping out the mishmash of styles and loud colours, she introduces a calmer range of warm neutral shades, unifying the interior by laying laminate flooring and creating a separate dining area(n)

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Property expert Ann Maurice offers advice to the owner of a four-bedroom Victorian villa in Garelochhead, Argyll and Bute. programme.desc: Prue decides Paul and Terese are too irresponsible to take care of Harlow. Amy's despair deepens as fallout from the sex tape continues(n)

programme.title: British Airways: 100 Years in the Sky
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part documentary charting the history of the airline from the first flight in 1919 to the present day. It begins with the early years of passenger air travel, and looks at the first companies and passengers who braved open cockpits, wicker chairs and no on-board toilets(n)

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of a high-speed police interception unit. Nick and the team perform a textbook tactical pursuit and containment on a suspected cloned car, and a drunk driver in Bradford attempts to escape the police. Meanwhile, officers look into the case of a hit and run in west Yorkshire(n)

programme.title: Police Raids: Caught by Surprise
programme.desc: Cameras follow enforcement agencies carrying out raids across Britain, as they take drastic action against fraudsters, counterfeiters and criminals. Police tackle the brothers responsible for a major drug-dealing operation in south Wales, while sniffer dogs Yoyo and Scamp track illegal and possibly dangerous cigarettes(n)

programme.title: Robbing Your Relatives
programme.desc: Return of the documentary taking a look at what can happen when families fall out over money. The first episode examines the case of a teenager cheated out of her grandfather's inheritance by her own dad and stepmother. The pair forged signatures and lied to solicitors and banks to steal the money, then frittered it away on luxuries and paying off their own debts. There is also the story of a pair of identical twins who fell out over a £55,000 loan, and a man who was alleged to have robbed his mother's pension and life savings, but took his own life before justice was done(n)

programme.title: Fare Dodgers: At War with the Law
programme.desc: A prolific offender is landed with an eyewatering bill to square off three years of illegal travel, while the police must step in when one suspect is found in possession of a banned substance. At one of London's busiest stations, vigilant inspectors Scott and Michelle have success intercepting repeat offenders, but will they catch a notorious passenger who the station staff report comes through their station each day?(n)

programme.title: Magnificent Obsession (1954)
programme.desc: A wealthy playboy blames himself for the death of the doctor who saved his own life in a boating accident, and when he later falls in love with the medic's widow and a second accident causes her to go blind, he trains as a surgeon in the hope of restoring her sight. Melodrama, starring Rock Hudson, Jane Wyman, Agnes Moorehead and Otto Kruger(n)

programme.title: The Bravados (1958)
programme.desc: A widower visits a town where the men he believes murdered his wife are about to be hanged. When they escape from jail, he takes the law into his own hands and decides to hunt down and kill the fugitives. But over time, he begins to question whether his actions are right. Western, starring Gregory Peck, Joan Collins and Stephen Boyd(n)

programme.title: Stalag 17 (1953)
programme.desc: The inmates of a German PoW camp are convinced there is a traitor in their midst when the guards seem to be forewarned of their escape attempts. An unassuming sergeant content to remain a captive until the end of the conflict becomes the focus of suspicion as mob mentality takes hold. Billy Wilder's Second World War drama, starring an Oscar-winning William Holden, with Don Taylor, Otto Preminger and Harvey Lembeck(n)

programme.title: Exodus: Gods and Kings (2014)
programme.desc: Moses, the adoptive brother of the pharaoh of Egypt, is sent into exile, where he receives a message from God demanding he set all the country's slaves free. When his brother refuses to comply, the kingdom is struck by a series of devastating plagues. Biblical epic, starring Christian Bale, Joel Edgerton, Sigourney Weaver and John Turturro(n)

programme.title: Miss Peregrine's Home for Peculiar Children Interview Special
programme.desc: Director Tim Burton and stars Eva Green, Asa Butterfield and Ella Purnell talk about making the new fantasy adventure(n)

programme.title: Blades of Glory (2007)
programme.desc: A bad-boy figure skater and his effeminate arch-enemy are banned from the sport after their intense rivalry results in a brawl. However, the pair find a loophole in the regulations allowing them to enter the world championships as the first ever same-sex pair - if they can put aside their differences long enough to compete. Comedy, starring Will Ferrell, Jon Heder, Will Arnett and Amy Poehler(n)

programme.title: Transporter 3 (2008)
programme.desc: Underworld courier Frank Martin's latest assignment involves transporting the kidnapped daughter of a crooked government minister across Europe on behalf of an eco-terrorist faction. However, a device will kill him if he leaves his car, thugs employed by the politician are out to murder him, and his passenger has a bad case of Stockholm syndrome. Action thriller sequel, with Jason Statham and Natalya Rudakova(n)

programme.title: 20 Feet from Stardom (2013)
programme.desc: Oscar-winning documentary exploring the lives and careers of backing singers. Performers from various genres and eras share their personal accounts of working in the shadows of famous artists and the rewards and sacrifices that their role brings. With contributions from singers including Mick Jagger, Stevie Wonder and Bruce Springsteen(n)

programme.title: 3:10 to Yuma (1957)
programme.desc: A farmer in need of money agrees to guard a notorious outlaw until the train arrives to transport him to jail. Meanwhile, the pair are holed up in a hotel, where his prisoner plays mind games with him - and the villain's cohorts are waiting outside ready to engineer an escape. Western adapted from a novel by Elmore Leonard, with Van Heflin, Glenn Ford, Felicia Farr and Leora Dana(n)

programme.title: 40 Guns to Apache Pass (1967)
programme.desc: A cavalry captain sets out to deliver a shipment of rifles to a fort besieged by marauding Apaches, but the mission is undermined by a villainous corporal who plans to steal the weapons and sell them to the enemy. Western, starring Audie Murphy, Michael Burns, Laraine Stephens, Kenneth Tobey and Michael Keep(n)

programme.title: Angel and the Badman (1947)
programme.desc: A gunslinger determined to track down his father's killer is badly wounded and taken in by a Quaker family, whose daughter nurses him back to health. His attraction to the girl and growing respect for the family's peaceful way of life lead him to consider renouncing violence - but his desire for revenge proves hard to surrender. Western, starring John Wayne, Bruce Cabot and Gail Russell(n)

programme.title: The Black Shield of Falworth (1954)
programme.desc: The son of a disgraced medieval nobleman is raised in poverty. With the support of a kindly earl, he gains the chance to train as a knight and reclaim his birthright, proving his worth by fighting to foil a plot to overthrow the king. Swashbuckling adventure, starring Tony Curtis, Janet Leigh, David Farrar and Barbara Rush(n)

programme.title: Knight and Day (2010)
programme.desc: A woman has a chance encounter with a rogue secret agent, who has been framed as a traitor and is on the run from his own superiors. Realising she has been mistaken for his accomplice and is in danger for knowing too much, the fugitive kidnaps her and drags her on a dangerous journey around the world, as he sets out to clear his name and protect a vitally important invention. Comedy adventure, with Tom Cruise, Cameron Diaz and Viola Davis(n)

programme.title: The Wolf of Wall Street (2013)
programme.desc: A New York stockbroker rises from unemployment to become one of the richest men in the city's financial elite through an illegal scheme to defraud investors. His wealth brings him an outrageously debauched lifestyle, but comes at a price as the FBI become suspicious of his manipulation of the markets. Martin Scorsese's fact-based drama, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Jonah Hill and Matthew McConaughey(n)

programme.title: Child 44 (2015)
programme.desc: A secret police agent in 1950s Russia is disgraced for refusing to denounce his own wife as a traitor. Reassigned to a remote outpost, he tries to bring a serial child murderer to justice, but faces an official cover-up by a rival, who wants to maintain the illusion that Soviet society is without crime. Detective thriller based on Tom Rob Smith's novel, starring Tom Hardy, Gary Oldman and Noomi Rapace(n)

programme.title: The Wonderful Country (1959)
programme.desc: A solitary gunman lies low for years in Mexico hiding out from the American authorities, until circumstances force him to return to the US - where he reluctantly agrees to take on dangerous work with a team of Texas Rangers patrolling the border. Western, starring Robert Mitchum, Julie London, Pedro Armendariz and Jack Oakie(n)

programme.title: A Man Alone (1955)
programme.desc: A gunman stumbles across the bodies of passengers murdered in a stagecoach robbery. Riding to the nearest town to report the crime, he finds himself accused of the killings and faces a battle to prove his innocence. Western, directed by and starring Ray Milland, with Raymond Burr, Lee Van Cleef, Mary Murphy and Ward Bond(n)

programme.title: Robinson Crusoe on Mars (1964)
programme.desc: Stranded on Mars with only a monkey as a companion, an astronaut has to quickly figure out how to survive a lack of oxygen, food and water on the lifeless planet. Sci-fi drama, inspired by Daniel Defoe's classic tale, starring Paul Mantee, Adam West and Victor Lundin(n)

programme.title: Decision at Sundown (1957)
programme.desc: A gunslinger rides into a backwoods town intent on finding his dead wife's former lover and exacting revenge for her death. The man he blames now has total control of the town and has the sheriff at his beck and call. Undeterred, the drifter offers his enemy an ultimatum - he has until sundown to leave the town or face retribution for his crimes. Western, with Randolph Scott, John Carroll and Andrew Duggan(n)

programme.desc: A hapless family's camping holiday in the mountains is brought to a sudden end when they accidentally hit the legendary Bigfoot with their car. They decide to adopt the endearing creature, but have more trouble than expected trying to keep him concealed and to avoid arousing their neighbours' suspicions. Comedy, starring John Lithgow, Melinda Dillon, David Suchet, Don Ameche and Kevin Peter Hall(n)

But will the family choose to up sticks or stay where they are?(n)

programme.desc: A woman travels all the way from Romania in the hope that Professor Noel Fitzpatrick can mend her dog Clifford's squashed spinal cord. A golden Labrador is offered a total shoulder replacement and a puppy faces losing two of its toes after a collision with a plant pot(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows Lysette and Nigel Offley, who are knocking down an old Thames boathouse and constructing a cutting-edge home in its place. Taking inspiration from the river itself, they have designed a building of waves and curves with a huge glass porthole for a roof light. But problems begin almost from the start, as they cannot find a contractor willing to build over the water, their architect leaves unexpectedly and the costs turn out much higher than expected. To make matters worse, the neighbours are not happy about such a radical sight in their traditional community. As they begin to compromise, Kevin wonders if the couple's dreams will ever be realised(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: At the end of a busy year at Chateau de la Motte-Husson, Dick and Angel throw a Christmas party for everyone who helped them in restoring the property to its current glory. The festive menu includes produce from their own walled garden, as well as homemade wines, and the couple also put up a Christmas tree which, as usual, is on a spectacular scale(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras reveal how individuals adapt and survive in the face of adversity. Subjects include Paul, 60, who is rushed to A&E after colliding with a van. Doctors are concerned that he may have serious injuries to his pelvis and spine. There's also a profile of four-year-old Jeremy, who is struggling to breathe. He suffers from a series of chronic conditions, including microcephaly and epilepsy(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Following the criminalisation of formerly legal highs, crack and heroin use is on the up in Wiltshire - and some of the users seem to be getting younger and a routine stop and search uncovers a bag of crack cocaine in the possession of a boy still in his teens. Meanwhile, PC Luke Hobbs leads his first drugs raid on a suspected heroin and crack supplier in Swindon, discovering the property has been commandeered by a dealer from London who is using the address as a base to make a move into the local market.(n)

Plus, Matt Tebbutt looks at the best and quickest ways to remove the shell from a hard-boiled egg(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team uncovers more unusual, intriguing and surprising secrets behind mass-produced food. Kate Quilton is in Darwin in Australia's Northern Territory to find out how crocodiles are farmed for their meat, while Jimmy Doherty travels to Grimsby and Iceland as he investigates whether It's possible to make oil from salmon or haddock livers instead of the more traditional cod. Plus, Matt Tebbutt examines why bagels taste so different to bread despite the ingredients being virtually the same(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin help Diane Rollisson and Robert Somerville, who want a holiday home in either Northumberland or the Midi-Pyrenees(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: A couple want a rural holiday home, but are divided as to where. John McMahon wants to buy a place in the Llyn Peninsula in north Wales, where his rowing club is based, while his wife Elaine would prefer the warm climate and cultured atmosphere of France's Cote d'Azur. Laura Hamilton and Adrian Simpson help them to resolve the dilemma(n)

programme.desc: The competition comes from in and around Belfast, where the first host is chatty PR manager Claire. She aims to impress with a menu made up of her favourite restaurant dishes, but never having plucked a pheasant before, she has a challenge on her hands. The guests she's hoping to impress are DJ Ryan, joiner and part-time inventor Neil, charity events manager Cailin, and window cleaning manager Paul, on a lively night filled with laughter, music and dancing(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.desc: The duo meet a couple of medics whose three-bedroom Victorian semi is no longer working for them and their two young daughters. programme.title: The Supervet

programme.desc: Mary Martin and Carl Turner want to build a home that resembles a giant stack of glass cubes in Brixton, south London. But to succeed, their finances must be pushed to their limit, so Carl takes on as many of the specialist jobs as he can to save money. Kevin McCloud follows their progress(n)

programme.title: History's Ultimate Spies
programme.desc: After the death of Cardinal Wolsey in 1530, Thomas Cromwell became Henry VIII's 'lap dog'. In order to do the king's bidding, he established a network of spies that steered Henry through his troubles with Rome, divorces, assassinations attempts and the establishment of a new religion. as this documentary reveals, though cunning and ambitious, none of these achievements proved sufficient to preserve his life(n)

programme.title: History's Ultimate Spies
programme.desc: As a Protestant, Queen Elizabeth I lived with the threat of assassination from Catholics throughout her reign. Thankfully, there was an army of men available to work in secret under spy master Francis Walsingham with the sole aim of protecting her. As this documentary reveals, Walsingham was able to demonstrate remarkable cunning in his role(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on an expedition along Dorset's Jurassic coast to explore the county's hidden Second World War story. Starting beside the defences on Chesil Beach, he heads east to finish in Swanage and Studland Bay. On his five-day walk he encounters stunning scenery and uncovers wartime acts of ingenuity and bravery. He also discovers the crucial part a swannery played in developing a secret weapon and reveals the role of Dorset in protecting Britain from invasion during the conflict(n)

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Madagascar
programme.desc: David Attenborough observes the wildlife of the desert-like west and south of the island, an area where dry spells can last months and survival is a struggle. Verreaux's sifakas live among trees with potentially lethal spikes, while the baobab trees house mouse lemurs, primates that feed on the droppings of fluffy bugs. During the toughest time of year, gangs of ring-tailed lemurs try to raise their young, and as the rains arrive, the year-long life cycle of the Labord's chameleon begins(n)

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the life and work of people in the polar regions, including the residents of Norilsk in Siberia, the coldest city on Earth. He also discovers how the Danish armed forces maintain their country's claim to Greenland's natural resources by mounting a 2,000-mile dog sled patrol during the winter, and finds out how a research base at the South Pole is able to cope with being cut off from the outside world for six months at a time(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The Three Gorges Dam, built across China's Yangtze river, which is the largest and heaviest concrete structure ever built, housing the world's largest power station(n)

programme.desc: Hitler's vision of the ideal citizen of the Third Reich, using breeding programmes and special schools to promote Aryan racial traits and complete devotion to his regime(n)

programme.title: History's Ultimate Spies
programme.desc: As a Protestant, Queen Elizabeth I lived with the threat of assassination from Catholics throughout her reign. Thankfully, there was an army of men available to work in secret under spy master Francis Walsingham with the sole aim of protecting her. As this documentary reveals, Walsingham was able to demonstrate remarkable cunning in his role(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on another expedition through some of Britain's most historic landscapes. The moors and valleys of West Yorkshire were the home of, and inspiration for, the Brontes, the literary family who produced such classics as Wuthering Heights and Jane Eyre. Over four days, Tony heads out from the Victorian wool capital of Bradford and treks in a giant loop around what is now known as Bronte Country. From the birth of Charlotte, Emily, Anne and their brother Branwell in the suburb of Thornton, he traces their childhoods to the much-romanticised Bronte hub of Haworth, finding that the harsh reality of life in the 1820s was far removed from the fantasy world dreamed up by the children at their father's parsonage home(n)

programme.desc: It was 30 years after the Romans invaded Britain that they were ready to take on the challenge of conquering the Lake District. With the toughest landscape they had encountered in the nation, peopled by a rebellious tribe, it was no small task, but within a few years, the whole of Lakeland was under their control. Tony Robinson tackles the journey by himself, walking 50 miles from Penrith to Ambleside and on to the Irish Sea at Ravenglass, discovering that the Romans encountered beauty and danger in equal measure(n)

Prince Charles and other members of the royal family share their stories, providing insights into the Queen's life over an important and busy 12 months filled with celebrations. programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Medical journalist Michael Mosley and a team of doctors offer advice on health, using their expertise and the latest research to investigate the truth behind various claims being made in the media. Surgeon Gabriel Weston takes part in an experiment at the University of Surrey's Sleep Research Centre to discover whether an extra hour in bed could have major benefits, while A&E specialist Dr Saleyha Ahsan asks if people can be overweight and still healthy, and tests the accuracy of two different methods used to assess fatness(n)

programme.desc: Dallas Campbell examines films from the Horizon archive to discover what science can teach people about dogs. programme.title: Traders: Millions by the Minute
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary going inside the fast and fiercely competitive world of financial trading, meeting the men and women who play the markets in big cities around the world. New York-based Karen juggles two sets of twins, a busy social calendar and a $200million hedge fund. Bob has spent more than three decades jostling for position on Chicago's cattle futures trading floor, while London day traders Will and Piers use their expertise to train others in the art of making money from tiny moves in the markets. Narrated by Saskia Reeves(n)
